data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260508T015435-c025f627/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260508T015435-c025f627/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260508T015435-c025f627/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260508T015435-c025f627/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260508T015435-c025f627/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-a1ae37ff">
    <title>agent: verify scoped mechanical --no-verify tracker commits</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Mechanical `--no-verify` tracker commits previously committed unrelated staged files when the index already contained junk from a failed broad checkpoint. Current code appears to have the path-scoping fix, but the open bead still asks for old test names and old line refs. That stale prompt can cause a worker to duplicate or churn already-correct code.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1433-1496 defines `CommitTracker`/`commitTrackerLocked`; the actual commit is now `git commit --no-verify --only -m <msg> -- .ddx/beads.jsonl` at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1495.
- cli/internal/git/autocommit.go:76-82 builds mechanical commit args and adds `--only -- <basename>` whenever `IncludeStaged` is false.
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go:111 defines `TestTrackerCommit_OnlyCommitsTrackerPath`.
- cli/internal/git/autocommit_test.go:39 defines `TestAutoCommit_TargetOnlyPreservesUnrelatedStagedChanges`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Treat this bead as verification and closeout unless the assertions above no longer hold.
- Keep `--no-verify` for mechanical tracker commits, but ensure every default tracker/autocommit path is path-scoped and preserves unrelated staged files.
- Preserve the `IncludeStaged` escape hatch in AutoCommit for call sites that intentionally want a full-index commit; document/test that this is not the default tracker path.
- If the focused tests pass and broad package gates are green, close the bead instead of reimplementing the same changes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rewrite history to remove the old 12MB blob.
- Do not change checkpoint `SynthesizeCommit` broad staging behavior in this bead.
- Do not move tracker writes into worker worktrees; that is covered by separate evidence/coordinator architecture beads.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-32b3008e. No deps.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go keeps the CommitTracker commit path-scoped with `git commit --no-verify --only ... -- .ddx/beads.jsonl`.
2. cli/internal/git/autocommit.go keeps AutoCommit path-scoped by default with `--only -- <basename>` when `IncludeStaged` is false.
3. TestTrackerCommit_OnlyCommitsTrackerPath passes and proves unrelated staged files remain staged and are not included in the tracker commit.
4. TestAutoCommit_TargetOnlyPreservesUnrelatedStagedChanges passes and proves AutoCommit preserves unrelated staged files by default.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/git/... -run "TestTrackerCommit_OnlyCommitsTrackerPath|TestAutoCommit_TargetOnlyPreservesUnrelatedStagedChanges" -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/git/... ./cmd/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:git, kind:bug, reliability, prevention, observed-failure, verification</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-32b3008e" base-rev="2983b65446d083fe88271d17833049852e33dc14" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260508T015435-c025f627"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All ACs verified against existing code:

- **AC1**: `execute_bead.go:1495` — `git commit --no-verify --only -m <msg> -- .ddx/beads.jsonl` ✓
- **AC2**: `autocommit.go:78-81` — appends `--only -- <basename>` when `IncludeStaged` is false ✓
- **AC3**: `TestTrackerCommit_OnlyCommitsTrackerPath` passes, proves unrelated staged files stay staged ✓
- **AC4**: `TestAutoCommit_TargetOnlyPreservesUnrelatedStagedChanges` passes, proves default path-scoping ✓
- **AC5**: Focused test run green ✓
- **AC6**: Broad package suite (`./internal/agent/... ./internal/git/... ./cmd/...`) green ✓
- **AC7**: `lefthook run pre-commit` passes ✓

The bead is a verification/closeout bead — the fix was already in place. No code changes required. `no_changes_rationale.txt` written with `verification_command` for the loop to close it.
